objective_vector:
SEVEN measured axes off DynamicMCResult, macrostate_v2 namespace (emit Pc/Fw/Fs/Fd or macro_* keys, never bare). TWO tiers.

GATE TIER (reliability + finish-rate; NEVER tradeable Pareto axes):
- macro_Fw=basin_F_wrong, macro_Fs=basin_F_split, macro_Fd=basin_F_deadline, plus finished_fraction. Absolute budgets eps_w=eps_s=1e-3, eps_d=1e-2 are structurally infeasible in mm_high (EV10: Fw~0.02-0.05 for ALL policies incl distance) so the literal budget gives an EMPTY feasible set. Operational gate = MATCHED-TO-DISTANCE on ALL THREE failure modes (Fw, Fs, Fd) -- Fd is added to the gate (reviewer leak fix): a latency win bought by MORE deadline misses (the survivors finish faster) must be blocked, and Fd is infeasible-for-all exactly like Fw, so it belongs in the gate not as a visible-only column.

FREE PARETO AXES (compared ONLY inside the matched-Fw/Fs/Fd AND matched-Pc feasible set):
1. Pc=macro_P_correct (basin_P_correct), MAXIMISE -- primary, must be CI-equal-or-better (equivalence-gated, margin below).
2. lat_basin = basin_tau_correct_mean (the network participation-weighted basin first-hit epoch over CORRECT trials; * poll_window_s for seconds), MINIMISE. THIS IS THE LATENCY AXIS -- pinned to the basin macrostate metric, NOT mean_finalisation_time and NOT per-node rounds_to_decide (reviewer 'three latency measures' fix).
3. lat_cvar = latency_cvar (CVaR worst-tail per-trial elapsed, ALL trials, horizon-censored), MINIMISE.
4. energy_mean = mean_energy (per-trial tx energy, ALL trials), MINIMISE.
5. energy_cvar (CVaR tail energy), MINIMISE.
energy_budget stays report-only (math.inf). PRE-REGISTERED PHYSICS PRIOR: distance=poll-nearest=shortest-link=lowest per-poll tx energy almost by construction, so distance is the near-certain energy MINIMISER; energy is reported but the LIVE axes where dominance can flip are lat_basin and lat_cvar. CVaR fix: dynamic_mc hardcodes cvar_level=0.9 not the profile's 0.95 -- so REPORT the axis honestly as CVaR_0.9 (do NOT claim it matches service_profile.latency_quantile), OR one-line patch cvar_level=profile.latency_quantile; pick one and state it in the manifest. So: feasible-set-first (matched Fw/Fs/Fd + matched Pc), then 4-axis latency/energy Pareto.
reliability_handling:
Lexicographic three-stage gate; reliability is a GATE not an axis (EV12 mechanism promoted to the comparison law). ALL gate decisions use SAMPLE-SPLIT trials (a SEPARATE generator seed block from the Stage-2 latency/energy estimation trials) so admission does not condition-bias the comparison CIs (reviewer sample-split fix). All gate tests are PAIRED under CRN and EQUIVALENCE-MARGINED (no bare overlap fallacy).

STAGE 0 -- RELIABILITY GATE: candidate A admitted vs distance D iff, on the gate-split trials, the seed/scene-level bootstrap CI of the PAIRED difference is non-inferior on EACH of Fw, Fs, Fd: Delta=axis(A)-axis(D), admit iff CI-upper(Delta) <= margin AND point-estimate Delta <= margin (BOTH the CI and the point must clear it -- closes the 'point-worse but CI overlaps' laundering hole). Margins: m_Fw=m_Fd=0.003 (~10pct of the 0.02-0.05 operating level), m_Fs via zero-failure Wilson UCB <= eps_s=1e-3. Drop every 'CIs overlap' disjunct. Policies failing Stage 0 are EXCLUDED -- their Pc/latency advantage is reliability-bought (the un-gated +0.10 oracle and +0.03 distilled GNN are INADMISSIBLE; demonstrating their rejection is itself a result confirming EV12).

STAGE 1 -- Pc EQUIVALENCE GATE: among Stage-0 survivors, A's latency/energy win counts only if Pc(A) is non-inferior to Pc(D): paired Delta_Pc CI LOWER bound > -m_Pc with m_Pc=0.01 (TOST-style; CI inside the margin, not merely overlapping 0). Reason (real confound): lat_basin is over CORRECT runs only, so a policy abandoning hard nodes finishes easy ones faster -- a phantom latency win that is really lower Pc. Matched-Pc with a margin kills it.

STAGE 2 -- PARETO over the 4 latency/energy axes on the (matched-reliability, matched-Pc) set, using the SEPARATE estimation-split trials.

Report Fw/Fs/Fd + finished_fraction + censored-fraction with CIs as a visible audit column beside every verdict (A0: reliability never folded into a denominator). 'Distance is dominated' = precise/falsifiable: some admissible policy is CI-strictly-better (post-Holm, outer CI) on >=1 of {lat_basin, lat_cvar, energy_mean, energy_cvar} and non-inferior (within margin) on the rest.
measurement_cut:
LEAD WITH THIS -- NO new training. run_dynamic_mc returns the full vector for ANY policy; the whole surface is one measurement sweep. Run it FIRST; only train oracles if it exposes an exploitable latency axis.

Policies measured (full vector each): distance (FROZEN beta, see pitfall -- beta selected on TRAIN scenes, single value, NOT per-scene re-tuned), uniform_esp, link_quality, load_balanced, region_bridge, trained-GNN (5 EV6 checkpoints mcf_seed*_steps150.pt, as-is). The per-scene MC oracle is measured as a FRONTIER REFERENCE only and is EXCLUDED from the dominance estimator (reviewer fix: it sees scene correctness structure, can never deployably dominate; running the paired-Delta test on it invites the EV12 misread).

CRN with VERIFICATION (reviewer desync fix): one shared torch.Generator seed per scene across ALL policies, but CRN only reliably pairs the POLICY-INDEPENDENT evidence draw (ev.sample, line 225, the dominant correctness driver); the per-poll u-draws (line 283) and subset draws desync across policies with different samplers. MITIGATION: draw evidence from a DEDICATED sub-generator seeded per (scene,trial-block) so the evidence realisation is provably common across policies; EMPIRICALLY VERIFY by running two policies and asserting their sampled ev.correct tensors match. Pairing on the common evidence is what gives the variance reduction; do not assume full-trajectory pairing.

Two trial blocks per (policy,scene): a GATE-split block and an ESTIMATION-split block, distinct seeds (sample-split). 3000 trials each block (6000 total/cell). The INNER 4000/6000-trial paired CI is a WITHIN-SCENE DIAGNOSTIC ONLY -- do NOT report it as dominance evidence (drop the 'hw~0.008' selling point; it invites over-trust).

Aggregation: GNN = per-seed 5 observations; baselines = per-scene observations. The headline lives at the OUTER (seed/scene) level.

This single sweep = the surface.
surface_mapping:
Train scalarised oracles ONLY if Phase-1 shows distance off the latency frontier. CRITICAL CORRECTION to the synthesised design: ONLY Pc and Fw are sound PER-NODE rewards (reinforce_correct line 377, reinforce_wrong line 378). BOTH latency AND energy must be NETWORK-LEVEL per-trial credit -- the per-node latency surrogate (rounds_to_decide) is REJECTED for two confirmed reasons: (a) rounds_to_decide=small t_round on WRONG fast decisions (line 313-314 'newly' fires either colour), so it incentivises fast-wrong, fighting the Fw gate; (b) the true objective basin_tau_correct_mean is the NETWORK participation-weighted first-hit epoch, NOT a function of per-node taus -- minimising mean per-node tau can RAISE basin tau (decide peripherals fast, delay participation-heavy nodes). 'Train on surrogate, verify on macro' is insufficient.

SOUND LATENCY ORACLE (network-credit): the per-trial basin first-hit tau is ALREADY computed as bo['tau'] / bo['code'] inside basin_outcome_probabilities (first_hitting.py line 162) but NOT returned. One-line MC extension: expose reinforce_basin_tau = bo['tau'].detach() and reinforce_basin_code = bo['code']. Reward per trial R_t = -mu * (tau_t/R_d) MASKED to correct trials (code==0) only; trials that miss the basin get the fixed worst penalty tau=R_d+1, giving a clean decide-the-NETWORK-sooner-CORRECTLY signal. Credit to reinforce_logp.sum(dim=1) [T] with a per-trial scalar baseline R_t.mean(). This is the EXACT objective, not a surrogate.

ENERGY ORACLE (network-credit, confirmed correct): per-node energy is ILL-POSED -- cumulative_energy is per-trial [T] summed over nodes (line 327), MAC-coupled (energy_pn depends on the active set / interference). Use the per-trial scalar R_t = -nu*(energy_t/E_ref) credited to reinforce_logp.sum(dim=1) with its own baseline.

IMPLEMENTATION SOUNDNESS (reviewer malformed-gradient fix): you CANNOT add nu*energy or mu*tau inside the per-node loss of line 146. The reward has TWO estimators that must be SEPARATE loss terms summed at the loss level, each with its OWN baseline:
  loss = -mean_node[ omega*(correct - lambda_w*wrong - b_node).detach() * logp_node ]   (PER-NODE, line-146 form)
         - mean_trial[ (-(mu*tau_norm*[code==0]) - nu*energy_norm - b_trial).detach() * logp.sum(dim=1) ]   (NETWORK-credit)
where b_node is the per-node baseline (line 145) and b_trial is the per-trial scalar mean. NOT one fused per-node sum.

WEIGHTS / GRID (pruned to live axes): lambda_w PINNED PER REGIME so train-Fw <= distance (Stage-0-during-training, RE-FIT per regime -- do NOT import EV12's lambda=2 into R2 where the Fw-matching lambda differs). Corners on the LATENCY axis only (energy is distance's by physics): (lambda*,mu=0,nu=0)=Pc anchor, (lambda*,0.5,0), (lambda*,1.0,0), and ONE energy probe (lambda*,0,0.5) to confirm the physics prior. Normalise tau by R_d, energy by distance's energy_mean.

CONVERGENCE GUARDS (reviewer SNR fix): network-credit has higher variance than the per-node correct path. Use train_trials=600 (not 100) for the network-credit corners, init='distance', and the init='random' control; ACCEPT a corner only if its in-sample latency actually DROPS vs the Pc-anchor and the history plateaus -- else label 'optimiser failed to move the axis' (a degenerate hull would FALSELY confirm distance is on the frontier). steps=100, 2 train scenes. EVAL with reinforce=FALSE (judge-faithful deployable rollout; the reinforce path detaches log_weights so the rollout is identical -- assert this). Each corner = per-scene UPPER-BOUND point; convex hull = achievable diagonal frontier; LABEL every number upper-bound. Only a GNN distilled+tested on HELD-OUT scenes is a deployability claim.
gnn_placement:
Two placements, identical measurement to baselines, both gated by Stages 0-1.

1. AS-IS deployable (the honest fixed artefact): load each of 5 EV6 checkpoints mcf_seed*_steps150.pt, build its ESP policy, run_dynamic_mc gate+estimation blocks on the same scenes/CRN => per-seed full vectors, seed-level bootstrap outer CI. Per EV6/EV13 expect it AT distance on Pc at matched reliability (sec-13.2 parity), so the open question is purely whether it lands lower on lat_basin/lat_cvar at equal Pc+Fw+Fs+Fd. FAIRNESS CAVEAT (reviewer fix, state explicitly): the as-is GNN gets NO operating-point knob while distance got a frozen-but-tuned beta and the oracle got scalarisation corners. An as-is-GNN loss therefore certifies 'THIS CHECKPOINT is dominated', NOT 'no GNN can dominate' -- it is NOT evidence distance dominates the GNN policy class. (Optional symmetric knob: a logit-temperature sweep selected on TRAIN scenes, frozen, to match distance's tuning budget.)

2. DISTILLED to a non-dominated corner (tests GNN-reachability, CONTINGENT on Phase-2 finding a non-dominated latency corner): EV13 proved the GNN is CAPABLE (corr 0.86, generalises). Distil the GNN to that corner's oracle logits (per-node-centered MSE, EV13 protocol). CRITICAL EV13 BLIND-SPOT FIX: EV13 measured Pc ONLY and silently inherited the oracle's elevated Fw -- here the distilled GNN MUST be measured on the FULL vector incl Fw/Fs/Fd and pass Stage 0 before any latency win counts. Held-out scene count: EV13's 2 held-out scenes CANNOT satisfy the 'majority of scenes' outer rule, so the distilled-GNN result is DOWNGRADED to a directional/feasibility check, not a dominance claim, unless held-out scenes are raised to >=4.

GNN-vs-distance = the dominance verdict; GNN-vs-oracle-hull = the deployable-vs-upper-bound gap.
dominance_test:
Pairwise paired-CRN Pareto-dominance with CIs at matched reliability, EQUIVALENCE-MARGINED, sample-split, Holm-corrected. X dominates distance D iff, within the (Stage-0 matched-Fw/Fs/Fd AND Stage-1 matched-Pc) feasible set decided on the GATE split, X is non-inferior (within margin) on ALL 4 free axes AND CI-strictly-better on >=1, evaluated on the ESTIMATION split.

INNER estimator (within-scene diagnostic ONLY): under common-evidence CRN, paired per-trial differences d_run=axis(X)_run-axis(D)_run, bootstrap the mean paired difference. For the MEAN axes (lat_basin, energy_mean) CRN pairing cancels evidence noise. For the CVaR axes (lat_cvar, energy_cvar) the paired-mean argument DOES NOT transfer (reviewer fix): CVaR is a nonlinear tail functional, the worst-10pct sets differ between policies, so per-trial pairing does not cancel -- use a QUANTILE/CVaR-difference bootstrap (resample trials, recompute each policy's CVaR, difference) with its own variance, and its own power check. The lat_basin paired diff is defined ONLY on the BOTH-CORRECT intersection (reviewer fix: lat_basin conditions on per-policy-different correct subsets); report the intersection size and the conditioning explicitly; cross-check against the unconditional lat_cvar (over ALL trials) so a both-correct-set artefact is visible.

OUTER CI (the verdict lives HERE, A0): seed-level (GNN, n=5) / scene-level bootstrap. With only 4-6 units this is underpowered for a Holm-corrected 4-axis claim, so PRE-REGISTER the headline as the per-scene PAIRED SIGN TEST / direction-consistency (same sign across the MAJORITY of scenes) as PRIMARY evidence, with the bootstrap interval as secondary -- do NOT silently fall back to the demoted pooled-trial CI. To make a true population claim, raise scenes to >=8/regime.

MULTIPLICITY: pre-register the EXACT family (4 axes x admitted policies x 2 regimes) and the SINGLE primary contrast (as-is GNN vs frozen-distance on lat_basin in the loose-deadline R2). Holm/Bonferroni (alpha 0.05 / num strict tests) on the strict-better tests; a separation must SURVIVE family-wise correction. The non-inferiority gates use their pre-registered margins (not Holm). Reliability gate uses paired Delta CIs + Wilson zero-failure for Fs, NOT marginal-overlap.

PRE-REGISTERED VERDICT FUNCTION (reviewer fix): per regime -- (i) X dominates D iff strict-better (post-Holm, outer sign-test majority, CI excludes 0) on >=1 axis AND within-margin non-inferior on all 4 => 'distance DOMINATED'; (ii) D strict-better on >=1, X non-inferior elsewhere => D dominates; (iii) each strict-better on a different axis => legitimate Pareto tradeoff (report spread, NOT a win); (iv) all 4 axes within margin both ways => PARITY-extended-to-full-vector (EV12 generalised; the prior-likely outcome). MDE: pre-register the minimum operationally-meaningful latency gap (e.g. 1 epoch / X% of R_d) so a parity result is interpretable as no-effect, not under-power.
run_matrix:
PHASE 1 -- MEASUREMENT (no training; do FIRST):
- Regimes (compare WITHIN regime only -- lat conditioning shifts across regimes): R1 = mm_high(base_err=0.35, corr=0.25), R_d=6, N=120, GRID=(5,5,3), PROTO k=3/alpha=2/beta=3 (EV12/EV13 deadline-dominated headline). R2 = same marginals, R_d=10, N=120 (LESS deadline-dominated -- where a latency edge is most plausible). FEASIBILITY GUARD: before running R2 assert snowball_layout(beta=3, r_max=10) validates (line 166) and pad.out_degree>=k (line 181); raise BOTH protocol_cfg.r_max AND service_profile.max_poll_epochs to 10 (two separate knobs -- a mismatch makes first-hitting use the wrong horizon). Drop R_d=14 unless R2 shows signal (compute).
- Scenes: TRAIN scenes [32,33] (knob selection: distance beta, oracle lambda, GNN temperature -- all frozen here). HELD-OUT/test scenes for dominance: [30,31] plus add [34,35] to reach 4 (EV13 held-out + 2) -- aim 6-8 if compute allows for the outer CI.
- Trials: 3000 GATE-split + 3000 ESTIMATION-split per (policy,scene); evidence from a dedicated common sub-generator (verified-paired); uniform_participation.
- Policies (dominance set, 5+5): distance(frozen beta from TRAIN-scene Pc-at-matched-Fw argmax over {0.02,0.04,0.06,0.08}), uniform_esp, link_quality, load_balanced, region_bridge, trained-GNN x5 seeds (EV6 steps150). PLUS MC free-edge oracle (per-regime-refit lambda) as FRONTIER REFERENCE, NOT in the dominance test.
- Output (hash-bound, macrostate_v2 keys, manifest w/ git_commit + profile config_hash + cvar_level note + CRN-verification result): full 7-vector + Fw/Fs/Fd + finished/censored fraction + Wilson per-axis + seed/scene-bootstrap paired-Delta-vs-distance CIs + per-scene sign-test, per cell.

PHASE 2 -- CONDITIONAL ORACLE MAPPING (training; ONLY if Phase-1 shows distance off lat_basin or lat_cvar -- if Phase-1 shows distance on the 4-axis frontier at matched Pc/reliability, that DIRECTLY answers the question (distance = multi-objective ceiling, EV12 extended) and Phase-2 is unnecessary -- pre-register this as the likely primary outcome):
- Extend run_dynamic_mc to return reinforce_basin_tau=bo['tau'].detach() + reinforce_basin_code=bo['code'].
- Corners {(lambda*,0,0),(lambda*,0.5,0),(lambda*,1.0,0),(lambda*,0,0.5)}; lambda* re-pinned per regime so train-Fw<=distance. SEPARATE per-node and network-credit loss terms (each own baseline). steps=100, train_trials=600 (network-credit SNR), init in {distance,random}.
- Convergence guard: accept a corner only if in-sample latency drops vs Pc-anchor + history plateaus; else flag optimiser failure.
- Eval each corner FULL vector at gate+estimation blocks on >=4 held-out scenes, reinforce=False; build hull = achievable diagonal frontier; overlay distance + GNN.
- Distil GNN from any non-dominated corner (EV13 protocol, >=4 held-out); re-measure FULL vector incl Fw/Fs/Fd; apply Stage 0-1. Downgrade to directional if held-out < 4.

Per admitted winner: re-cert Fs via zero-failure Wilson UCB + paired Delta-Fw/Fd vs distance (matched, margined, not absolute).
top_pitfalls: THE SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT FAIRNESS PITFALL -- FROZEN, TRAIN-SELECTED KNOBS WITH EQUAL BUDGET FOR ALL POLICIES. Distance must NOT pick its beta per-scene/per-axis post-hoc (a moving target that makes it un-dominatable by construction); select ONE beta on TRAIN scenes [32,33] (argmax mean Pc at matched-Fw), FREEZE it, evaluate dominance ONLY on held-out scenes. The oracle lambda and any GNN temperature are selected the SAME way, same budget, no more. Tuning distance on the test scenes fits the baseline to the test; un-tuning distance while the oracle/GNN get per-scene fitting is a reverse-strawman.

RELIABILITY-BOUGHT WINS (EV12 trap): never compare Pc/latency/energy before Stage-0 matched-Fw/Fs/Fd gating. Gate on the PAIRED Delta CI AND the point estimate (both must clear the margin) -- drop every 'CIs overlap' marginal disjunct (overlapping marginal CIs can still differ; CRN makes the paired test far more powerful). The un-gated oracle +0.10 and distilled-GNN +0.03 are INADMISSIBLE.

F_deadline MUST BE IN THE STAGE-0 GATE, not a visible-only column (concrete leak). Fd is infeasible-for-all per EV10 exactly like Fw; lat_basin conditions on correct/finished runs, so a latency win bought by MORE deadline misses (survivors finish faster) slips straight through unless Fd is matched-to-distance.

EVERY GATE IS AN EQUIVALENCE CLAIM AND NEEDS A PRE-REGISTERED MARGIN. 'No-worse'/'matched-Pc'/'matched-Fw' are non-inferiority claims; without a TOST margin under-powering trivially satisfies them and manufactures false dominance. Margins: m_Pc=0.01, m_Fw=m_Fd=0.003, m_Fs via zero-failure Wilson. CI must sit INSIDE the margin, not merely overlap 0.

ONLY Pc AND Fw ARE SOUND PER-NODE REWARDS. The per-node latency surrogate (rounds_to_decide) is REJECTED: it rewards fast-WRONG decisions (line 313-314 fires either colour) and optimises mean per-node tau, NOT the network basin_tau objective. BOTH latency and energy oracles must be NETWORK-LEVEL per-trial credit. The clean latency signal is bo['tau'] masked to code==0 (correct trials), credited to reinforce_logp.sum(dim=1) with a per-trial baseline.

PER-NODE ENERGY IS ILL-POSED -- do not fabricate it. cumulative_energy is per-trial [T] summed over nodes (line 327), MAC-coupled. Energy oracle = network-credit. AND the two estimators (per-node correct/wrong, network-credit tau/energy) are SEPARATE loss terms each with its OWN baseline summed at the loss level -- NOT one fused per-node sum (malformed-gradient bug).

OUTER CI HAS ONLY 4-6 SCENES / 5 SEEDS -- under-powered for a Holm-corrected 4-axis claim. Pre-register the per-scene paired SIGN TEST (majority direction-consistency) as PRIMARY, bootstrap interval secondary; NEVER fall back to the demoted pooled-trial Wilson which conflates model-init variance with within-MC Bernoulli noise. Raise scenes to >=8/regime for a population claim. Report an MDE so a parity result is no-effect, not under-power.

SAMPLE-SPLIT THE GATE FROM THE COMPARISON. Decide Stage-0/1 admission on a SEPARATE trial seed block from the trials used for the Stage-2 latency/energy CIs; reusing trials makes the post-gate comparison conditionally biased.

CRN ONLY RELIABLY PAIRS THE EVIDENCE DRAW. Different policies desync the u-draws/subset-draws; only the policy-independent evidence (ev.sample) is common. Draw evidence from a dedicated common sub-generator and EMPIRICALLY VERIFY the ev.correct tensors match across two policies before trusting the variance reduction. The CVaR axes' paired-mean argument does NOT transfer (nonlinear tail) -- use a CVaR-difference bootstrap.

PER-SCENE ORACLE = UPPER BOUND, EXCLUDED FROM THE DOMINANCE TEST (frontier reference only) and RE-FIT per regime (do not import EV12's lambda=2 into R2 -- the Fw-matching lambda differs, silently smuggling a reliability-bought win into the regime under test). Pin cvar_level honestly (hardcoded 0.9, not profile 0.95). Energy is distance's by physics (shortest links = lowest tx energy) -- focus power on the latency axes. An as-is-GNN loss certifies THIS CHECKPOINT, not the GNN policy class. Eval all oracles with reinforce=False (judge-faithful).
